title: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket is expected to crash into the moon this August - Engadget
summary: That's because even though it's larger than most natural lunar impactors, it's a hollow shell and is moving "much more slowly." Regardless, it's a chance for astronomers to observe an artificial impact in real time.
